claim_boundary: This is a local time-to-answer result, not a reproduction of the paper's headline practical-advantage claim.
The chi=256 MPS baseline did not establish full convergence.
The fast Majorana route demonstrates that observable-specific classical methods can change the ranking.

classical_baselines.method: Local quimb MPS with maximum bond dimension chi=256
classical_baselines.scope: End-to-end local wall time for the 120-qubit, 60-site reference
classical_baselines.status: not fully converged; maximum bond reached the requested cap
faster than the quantum proxy but visibly inaccurate
close to the chi=256 value for this selected observable

comparison.headline: The quantum execution proxy was 272.50x shorter than the local chi=256 MPS wall time for this declared instance.
comparison.scope: The ratio compares a quantum execution proxy with local MPS wall time; it is not full workflow parity and not a comparison with the later four-H200 classical implementation.

quantum.result_summary: The hardware produced a full 120-qubit observable profile; raw mean double occupancy was 0.22862549 and readout-corrected mean double occupancy was 0.23067722.

timings.scope: Estimated main plus readout circuit execution; excludes full human and cloud workflow time

entries.summary: A 60-site Fermi-Hubbard hardware workflow produced local charge, spin, and double-occupancy observables and was compared with local MPS and observable-specific Majorana calculations.
entries.title: Fermi-Hubbard dynamics on 120 qubits

claim_boundary: Hardware-only time is not cloud wall time and excludes several service overheads.
The local scalar normalization remains distinct from the tracker's published hadron scalar.
The result supports runtime separation and circuit or sector validation, not an independent precision reproduction of every published observable.

classical_baselines.method: Local Aer MPS on compiled QASM

Circuit-level tensor check on the same compiled-QASM family

comparison.headline: Both the local circuit checks and the paper-native baselines show a substantial runtime separation under their declared timing definitions.
comparison.scope: The local quantum number is hardware-only time, while the paper reports a separate 20-second QPU timing; the two timing systems must not be merged into one universal ratio.

official_sources.label: Ilcic et al., Observation of Robust and Coherent Non-Abelian Hadron Dynamics on Noisy Quantum Processors

quantum.backend: IBM hardware through Q-CTRL Fire Opal
quantum.result_summary: The local hardware route produced charge-sector and differential-observable data for the 120-qubit circuit family.

timings.scope: Local hardware time including readout circuits; excludes queue, API, compilation, and local analysis

entries.summary: A Loop-String-Hadron implementation follows a differential hadron signal on a 60-site lattice and compares the quantum route with local circuit-MPS checks and published tensor-network and Pauli-propagation baselines.
entries.title: Non-Abelian SU(2) hadron dynamics on 120 active qubits

claim_boundary: The classical calculation did not converge and no matched-accuracy ratio was obtained.
This is a tracker-compatible 80-qubit extension with N_init=8, not an official tracker instance or an N_init=500 reproduction.
The observation is local and does not cover every classical implementation or optimized compute platform.

classical_baselines.method: Tracker-linked Heisenberg BP-TN at bond dimension 16
classical_baselines.status: not converged; apparent ratio is not a valid physical estimate

classical_baselines.method: Tracker-linked Heisenberg BP-TN delta half at bond dimension 32
classical_baselines.status: not converged; value shifted by 86 percent from bond dimension 16

classical_baselines.method: Tracker-linked Heisenberg BP-TN delta half at bond dimension 64
classical_baselines.scope: Delta half only, stopped at the bounded wall-time limit

comparison.headline: The incomplete bond-dimension-64 classical delta half alone exceeded the complete Fire Opal action by more than 2.75x on this machine.
comparison.scope: The classical route did not converge and did not complete the delta0 half, so this is a local runtime lower bound rather than a matched-accuracy quantum-advantage result.

quantum.backend: IBM Kingston through Q-CTRL Fire Opal
quantum.result_summary: The measured delta/delta0 OLE ratio was 0.74028847 +/- 0.01663657; all eight sample ratios were positive.

timings.scope: Complete Fire Opal action wall time for all sixteen mitigated circuits

quantum.workflow: Sixteen mitigated circuits with N_init=8 and 8,000 shots per circuit

entries.summary: A tracker-compatible 80-qubit extension estimates an Operator Loschmidt Echo from finite computational-basis samples and compares the complete mitigated hardware action with a bounded tracker-linked BP-TN calculation.
entries.title: Operator Loschmidt Echo on 80 qubits

claim_boundary: The 70-qubit classical runtime is extrapolated from measurements ending at 12 qubits, not measured at full width.
The quantum samples have no validated full-distribution fidelity, and the separate predeclared 95 percent stabilizer test failed.
The post-hoc 75 percent lower bound is an exploratory sensitivity result, not 75 percent fidelity and not evidence of quantum advantage.

classical_baselines.method: Local Qiskit Aer extended-stabilizer fit evaluated at 70 qubits
classical_baselines.scope: Projected time for one sample from timings measured only at 4, 6, 8, 10, and 12 qubits
classical_baselines.status: extrapolated; not measured at 70 qubits and not quality matched

classical_baselines.method: Local ITensorMPS at maximum bond dimension 64
classical_baselines.scope: Warm-process state construction plus 256 samples for the complete 70-qubit circuit
classical_baselines.status: completed but strongly truncated and not converged

classical_baselines.method: Local exact MPS anchor at 14 induced qubits
classical_baselines.scope: Median runtime for a newly induced 14-qubit circuit
classical_baselines.status: exact small-width validation; not a 70-qubit baseline

comparison.headline: Hardware returned 256 samples in 19 quantum-seconds, while a local Aer fit projects about 6.89 million years for one 70-qubit sample; sample counts and output quality are not matched.
comparison.scope: This is a same-width clock scenario, not a measured speedup or a quality-matched quantum-advantage result.

quantum.backend: IBM Kingston through Q-CTRL Fire Opal and IBM Runtime
quantum.result_summary: The complete circuit returned 256 samples. The separate checked dataset retained 4,519 of 184,320 shots and gave a graph-state-prefix point estimate of 0.01217; its predeclared one-sided 95 percent lower-bound test failed.
quantum.task: Sample the complete random-graph circuit and estimate the graph-state prefix with predeclared stabilizers
